args.text: NOTE(mhroth): heavy uses an approximation of exp~ which may lead to slight inaccuracies at high midi notes
low-pass filter to smooth out the values
low-pass filter to smooth out the values
scale the FM depth to 1V/Oct so it matches standard VCV pitch depth
add the FM modulation to the main pitch
adc 1 -> v/oct input \, adc 2 FM CV input
The 'base_' prefix generates a large main knob. The '_adc1' suffix tells the UI generator to draw the adc~ 1 input jack directly underneath this knob.
The 'attenv_' prefix generates a small \, bipolar attenuverter knob. The '-1 1 0' sets its range. The '_adc2' suffix draws the adc~ 2 input jack directly below it.
multiply the incoming FM CV by the knob's value (-1 to 1)

How Columns Work: The text in the middle of your parameter name is the "core name". For example \, base_pitch_adc1 creates a column named PITCH. attenv_fm_adc2 creates a column named FM. Any parameters sharing the same core name will stack vertically in that column
We use [adc~] to receive actual audio/CV signals. The _adcN suffix in the parameter name only tells the layout script to draw that physical jack in the same vertical column as the knob.
scales Pd's ±1.0 to VCV's ±5.0V audio standard
